title: Four-seed RealMLP equal average
summary: Test a four-seed RealMLP equal average as a separate cost-versus-score branch from the two-seed parent.
rationale: If three seeds improve, four may improve further; if not, this branch gives a direct read on the saturation curve. It should remain a separate branch so its cost and benefit are measured cleanly.
implementation_hint: Start from the parent two-seed RealMLP average code. Add two additional RealMLP experts with the same parameters and folds but different seeds, then average all four probability vectors equally. Do not change features, n_ens, epochs, add CatBoost, or add stacking.
expected_effect: Improve OOF AUC if RealMLP seed averaging has not saturated by two seeds.
risk: Runtime roughly doubles relative to the two-seed parent and marginal gain may be below noise.
